§6Natural Gas§f

§aA gaseous byproduct of oil extraction with surprising versatility — from cordite to carbon dioxide!§f

Natural Gas is a flammable, combustible gas produced alongside crude oil when extracting with an §bOil Derrick§f or §bPumpjack§f. It is also obtained from §bBedrock Oil§f extraction using the §bHydraulic Fracking Tower§f. Each bucket provides §e50 kTU§f and §e75 kHE§f with a Gaseous fuel grade.

§6Production§f:
§b• Oil Derrick / Pumpjack§f (primary): Extracted as a byproduct alongside Crude Oil from standard oil deposits.
§b• Hydraulic Fracking Tower§f: Extracted from Bedrock Oil deposits alongside infinite Crude Oil.

§6Uses§f:
§b• Cordite§f (Chemical Plant): §e200mB§f + Niter + Sawdust + Sugar → §e4×§f Cordite.
§b• Carbon Dioxide§f (Chemical Plant): §e1,000mB§f Natural Gas → §e1,000mB§f CO₂.
§b• Hydrotreater§f: §e1,000mB§f Natural Gas + §e50mB§f Liquid Hydrogen → §e800mB§f Petroleum Gas + §e150mB§f Sour Gas.
§b• Pyrolysis Oven§f: §e12,000mB§f Natural Gas → §e8,000mB§f Liquid Hydrogen + §e1×§f Graphite Ingot.
§b• Pandemonium Processing§f: Natural Gas + Liquid Oxygen + Tantalite + Fluorite → rare metals solution.

§eTip§f: Natural Gas is renewable (extracted continuously with pumpjacks). It is inefficient to burn directly — convert it to §bPetroleum Gas§f via hydrotreating for far better value. The §bCordite§f recipe is particularly useful for early explosive ammunition.
